caseStudy.title: Data Warehouse Consolidation
caseStudy.description: Unifying 14 siloed analytics databases into a single platform.
caseStudy.overview: A media conglomerate operated 14 separate analytics databases accumulated through acquisitions, each with different schemas, access controls, and tooling. Cross-brand reporting required manual data reconciliation taking analysts days per report.
caseStudy.problem: Leadership had no unified view of business performance across brands. Analysts spent 60% of their time on data wrangling rather than analysis. Inconsistent metric definitions across brands made comparisons unreliable.
caseStudy.solution: A centralised cloud data warehouse was established as the single source of truth. Data from all 14 source systems was ingested via standardised connectors, with a semantic layer enforcing consistent metric definitions. Self-service BI tooling was rolled out with role-based access control to ensure data governance.

results.summary: Executives now access consistent, real-time performance dashboards across all brands. Analysts have shifted from data preparation to delivering strategic insights that drive business decisions.
